questions.question: Explain how string immutability in Python affects performance and memory management.
questions.answer: String immutability allows Python to perform string interning (reusing identical string literals in memory) and safely use strings as hash keys in dictionaries and sets. However, constructing strings via repeated concatenation in loops causes O(N^2) complexity due to repeated memory allocations. Using list appends + `''.join()` avoids this, achieving O(N) linear performance.
